text.tspan: Two souls, alas, are housed within my breast,
And each will wrestle for the mastery there.
The one has passion's craving crude for love,
And hugs to the world with eager, clinging
arms;The other, from earthly dust aspires,
Unto the high ancestral realms above.
Oh, there are moments when I dare not speak,
When the heart surges, yet the tongue is weak;
The spirit strains to break its mortal chain,
To taste the freedom heaven would ordain.
Yet still I linger, bound to mortal strife,
Half slave to flesh, half child of endless life;
Thus torn apart, my being cannot rest,
Till one strong voice subdues the other's quest.
